title: Hanuman Jayanti
intro: Hanuman Jayanti is one of the most important and sacred Hindu festivals celebrated to commemorate the birth of Lord Hanuman, the divine devotee of Lord Rama.
description:
Hanuman Jayanti is one of the most important and sacred Hindu festivals celebrated to commemorate the birth of Lord Hanuman, the divine devotee of Lord Rama. On this auspicious day, devotees worship Lord Hanuman and seek his blessings for strength, courage, protection, wisdom, and devotion. Hanuman Jayanti is celebrated in different regions of India on different dates, but it is most commonly observed on the full moon day of the Hindu month of Chaitra.

According to Hindu traditions, Lord Hanuman is considered an incarnation of Lord Shiva and is known for his unmatched strength, intelligence, humility, and unwavering devotion to Lord Rama. His selfless service and dedication played a significant role in the Ramayana. On Hanuman Jayanti, devotees visit temples, perform special prayers, recite Hanuman Chalisa, read Sundarkand, and participate in devotional bhajans and kirtans.

Devotees offer sindoor, jasmine oil, flowers, fruits, and sweets to Lord Hanuman. The festival inspires people to follow the path of devotion, courage, selfless service, humility, and righteousness.

Festival Month

Chaitra (March – April)
(In some regions, Hanuman Jayanti is also celebrated during Kartik month.)

Significance / Importance

Hanuman Jayanti holds great religious and spiritual significance in Hinduism. Lord Hanuman is worshipped as the symbol of strength, courage, devotion, wisdom, and protection from difficulties.

Devotees believe that worshipping Lord Hanuman removes fear, negativity, and obstacles while bringing confidence, strength, and success in life. The festival reminds devotees of Hanuman Ji’s pure devotion and selfless service toward Lord Rama.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early, take a holy bath, and wear clean clothes.
2. Clean the पूजा area and place an idol or image of Lord Hanuman.
3. Offer sindoor, jasmine oil, flowers, fruits, and sweets to Lord Hanuman.
4. Recite Hanuman Chalisa and Sundarkand.
5. Chant Hanuman Mantras with devotion.
6. Perform Hanuman Aarti.
7. Distribute Prasad and pray for strength, protection, peace, and happiness.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Hanuman Birth Katha
* Hanuman Chalisa
* Bajrang Baan
* Sundarkand
* Hanuman Mantra
* Hanuman Aarti
* Sankat Mochan Hanuman Ashtak

faq.a1: Hanuman Jayanti is generally celebrated on the full moon day of the Hindu month of Chaitra.
faq.a2: Hanuman Jayanti is celebrated to mark the birth anniversary of Lord Hanuman.
faq.q3: Which deity is worshipped on Hanuman Jayanti?
faq.a3: Lord Hanuman is worshipped on this sacred festival.
faq.q4: What offerings are made to Lord Hanuman on Hanuman Jayanti?
faq.a4: Devotees offer sindoor, jasmine oil, flowers, fruits, and sweets, especially laddus.
faq.q5: Which prayers are considered auspicious on Hanuman Jayanti?
faq.a5: Reciting Hanuman Chalisa, Sundarkand, Bajrang Baan, and Hanuman Mantras is considered highly auspicious.
faq.a6: Lord Hanuman was the greatest devotee of Lord Rama.
faq.q7: In which month is Hanuman Jayanti celebrated?
faq.a7: It is usually celebrated in March or April.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Hanuman Jayanti?
faq.a8: Hanuman Jayanti symbolizes devotion, strength, courage, humility, selfless service, and dedication to the path of righteousness.
